name: Carreiro da Senhora — circular route in Serra de Montejunto
name_original: Carreiro da Senhora — percurso circular na Serra de Montejunto
region_intro: Jogging routes around Cabanas de Torres offer a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by natural features and panoramic views. The region, situated in western Portugal, provides a mix of forest environments and trails within the Serra de Montejunto. These paths often feature varied terrain, including hills, making for engaging running experiences. Best jogging routes around Cabanas De Torres The most popular jogging route is Serra de Montejunto Windmills Trail, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 23 minutes to complete. This route offers views across the landscape, passing by windmills. Another top favourite among local runners is Quinta da Serra Trail, a difficult 3.0 miles (4.9 km) path. This trail features varied forest landscapes with moderate elevation changes. Local runners also love the Carreiro da Senhora — circular route in Serra de Montejunto, a 11.1 miles (17.9 km) trail leading through the Serra de Montejunto, often completed in about 2 hours 24 minutes. Jogging around Cabanas De Torres is defined by panoramic views, forest environments, and the hills of Serra de Montejunto. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Cabanas De Torres are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 190 runners have used komoot to explore Cabanas De Torres's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Cabanas De Torres?
A: There are 16 running routes around Cabanas De Torres that you can explore on komoot. These trails offer a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by natural features and panoramic views.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of running trails in Cabanas De Torres?
A: The majority of running trails in Cabanas De Torres are considered difficult, with 14 out of 16 routes falling into this category. There are also 2 moderate routes, but no easy options are listed. This suggests a more challenging experience for runners.

Q: Are there any circular running paths in Cabanas De Torres?
A: Yes, Cabanas De Torres offers several circular running paths. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381137991/carreiro-da-senhora-circular-route-in-serra-de-montejunto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carreiro da Senhora — circular route in Serra de Montejunto&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.1 miles (17.9 km) long. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381136513/carreiro-dos-ss-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carreiro dos SS Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 5.3 miles (8.5 km).

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on the running routes around Cabanas De Torres?
A: The running routes in Cabanas De Torres are defined by diverse natural scenery, including panoramic views and forest environments, particularly within the Serra de Montejunto. You'll encounter varied terrain, often with hills, providing an engaging experience.

Q: Are there any running trails that pass by interesting landmarks or viewpoints?
A: Yes, several trails offer access to interesting landmarks and viewpoints. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381122847/serra-de-montejunto-windmills-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Montejunto Windmills Trail&lt;/a&gt; offers views across the landscape, passing by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/760297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills of Serra de Montejunto&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6301182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Montejunto Watchtower&lt;/a&gt; for expansive views.

Q: What do other runners think about the trails in Cabanas De Torres?
A: The running routes in Cabanas De Torres are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 190 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, suggesting a positive experience overall.

Q: Are there any facilities like picnic areas near the running routes?
A: Yes, you can find facilities such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6301116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic Area of Serra de Montejunto&lt;/a&gt;, which could be a convenient spot for a break before or after your run. There's also the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5706330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montejunto Rural Campsite&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: Can I find trails that are suitable for a moderate running pace?
A: While most routes are challenging, there are a couple of moderate options available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381133431/quinta-da-serra-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quinta da Serra Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.0 miles (4.9 km) path featuring varied forest landscapes with moderate elevation changes, perfect for a less intense run.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of rivers or natural water features?
A: Yes, the region includes natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6302214" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ota River&lt;/a&gt;. While specific running routes directly alongside it aren't detailed, the Ota Karstic Canyon Trail is mentioned in the broader region, suggesting opportunities to experience such natural elements.

Q: What is the longest running route available in Cabanas De Torres?
A: The longest running route listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24527978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Meia Encosta Trail – Chapel of Saint John the Baptist loop from Paisagem Protegida da Serra de Montejunto&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 15.7 miles (25.2 km) and is considered difficult.

Q: Are there any historical or religious sites along the running paths?
A: Yes, you might encounter historical and religious sites. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24527978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Meia Encosta Trail&lt;/a&gt; includes a loop from the Chapel of Saint John the Baptist. Other nearby sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1760096" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel of Our Lady of the Snows (Ruins)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte da Ermida&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Very pleasant path that runs around Montejunto. It offers beautiful landscapes of the region. Some sections have loose stone floors, so it is worth paying attention.

At the top of the Montejunto mountain range, very close to the chapel of Nossa Senhora das Neves, there are remains of the first convent of the Order of São Domingos in Portugal. Initially built in 1218, shortly after Friar Soeiro Gomes arrived in Portugal, the current ruins are the result of the rebuilding (over the initial convent) that took place in the 18th century, upon the return of the Dominicans. Despite being in ruins, it is a grandiose monument that must be visited, with due care, given the dangers that ruins always contain.

Source: Natural.pt

Next to the monument, there is a trail called "Calçada dos Frades" that runs along the slope. This cobbled path, which is also thought to be from the 18th century, was opened to serve the construction and use of the Convent of Nossa Senhora das Neves. The path was also used by the friars to access the Real Fabrica do Gelo, located further down, where today there is a laser area and an interpretive center.

Located in the chestnut woods, it connects the Real Fábrica do Gelo complex and the Environmental Interpretation Centre. The Park has eight tables, with an average capacity of six to eight people per table. As support structures for its use, it has a small bar, toilets and a small playground.

Magnificently located in the Serra do Montejunto, famous for having the largest concentration of windmills in the Iberian Peninsula, Moinho de Avis is an absolutely unmissable windmill that dates back to 1810.

This typical windmill, with its clay pots set with sails, is located next to the Curral do Burro restaurant, providing the flour that is then used to make fresh bread, from the rare Barbela wheat.

Miguel Nobre, a carpenter by profession, is also a huge millology enthusiast and was responsible for the restoration of the Avis Mill in 2008. Restoring mills quickly became a full-time job.

Moinho de Avis is the largest windmill in Portugal.
